summary: Rev 1 plan review (non-authoring). All cited line numbers, landmark classes (router+shim=enforcement, MILESTONES=release), frozen-surface immunity (router outside the 55-file mirror), m65 helper feasibility (claude_run can drive the router), CI wiring, manifest dangling-reference logic, and the security posture VERIFIED correct. REJECT on two empirically-proven defects: B1 (high) — the plan requires `bash .harness/evidence/v011-verify.sh` => ALL PASS while the harness ALREADY fails 2 rows on untouched HEAD (v011:31 stale active-stop-block vs the evolved stop-verify-gate; v011:77 hardcoded 6-skills count vs 15 skill dirs) AND the plan forbids editing v011 — an unsatisfiable gate that would force laundering 2 failures; B2 (medium) — case-insensitivity covers the MATCHERS only; the TASK-EXTRACTION strips stay case-sensitive (router sed :77/:86, shim re.sub :69/:76), so the flagship mixed-case trigger fires but leaks the trigger token into the routed task string ('Run /dmc-ultrawork for: refactor this. DMC'), and NO planned check catches it (LP1 greps presence only; the leak is symmetric so cross-adapter equality still passes). Both are plan-text fixes, not redesigns.

criteria_checked.note: re-derived live: enforcement x2, release x1, ordinary rest
router not in ALL_LEGACY_FILES; only v0.2.8 pattern-matches the literal in task text
position-based anchors unaffected by -i; mid-sentence negative holds
TASK DMC-PLAN hits branch 2 not 3; tokens mutually exclusive
re.IGNORECASE identical for the Korean-prefix + ASCII-token shape
claude_run(:89) drives live hooks; c_prompt builds router JSON; has_context_of greps key only
B1 — harness already 39 PASS / 2 FAIL on untouched HEAD; plan demands ALL PASS + forbids editing it
B2 — strips stay case-sensitive; token leaks into the routed task; no planned probe detects it
additive emit text + matcher flag; no envelope change; grants correct; LP1 guard-safe

blockers.statement: Unsatisfiable REQUIRED gate: `bash .harness/evidence/v011-verify.sh` => ALL PASS demanded while the harness already fails 2 pre-existing non-router rows on HEAD 186ed8c (v011:31 stale stop-block; v011:77 6-skills hardcode vs 15) and the plan forbids editing v011.
blockers.suggested_fix: Reword the gate to assert ONLY the router-invariant rows (:59 dmc-ultrawork token, :60 dmc-plan-hard token, :62 mid-sentence negative, :63 parser line, :67-71 mode writes) remain PASS; record the 2 pre-existing failures as a documented known-baseline delta (mirroring the test-rollback 7/9 treatment).

blockers.statement: Case-insensitivity omits the task-extraction strips (router sed 's/[[:space:]]*dmc$//' :86 and dmc-plan :77; shim re.sub :76/:69) — a mixed-case trigger fires but the trigger token leaks into the routed task string; symmetric across adapters so parity-equality checks cannot catch it; LP1 only greps substring presence.
blockers.suggested_fix: Extend case-insensitivity to the strips PORTABLY (BSD sed lacks s///I: use character-class patterns [Dd][Mm][Cc] / [Dd][Mm][Cc]-[Pp][Ll][Aa][Nn]; shim adds flags=re.IGNORECASE to both re.sub calls) AND add an LP1 probe + m65 row asserting the emitted task equals the prompt WITHOUT the trigger token for a mixed-case input; or explicitly accept the leak with rationale (not recommended).

advisories.statement: Make the dmc-ultrawork/SKILL.md signature line UNCONDITIONAL so a direct /dmc-ultrawork slash invocation (no router emit) also opens with the signature — directive #2 plausibly covers it.

New CI-blocking m65 parity rows must use fresh per-prompt sandbox dirs (or explicit mode reset) — A13-A15 reuse one dir and a prior dmc/off write would bleed into the dmc-plan (mode-unchanged) case, making the blocking row flaky.
Name the HONEST_SCOPE caveat anchor (recommend the disclosed-residual-register section) so the one-liner lands discoverably.
List adapters/codex/README.md + adapters/claude-code/README.md as verified-no-edit in the scope ledger (their 'router'/'case-insensitive' mentions are generic/unrelated — verified benign).
